assertions.title: Mission worker agent has correct definition
assertions.reason: agents/mission-worker.md has the required frontmatter fields, required tool set, max_turns 100, timeout_mins 30, and body sections for startup, work, cleanup, and structured handoff.
evidence: agents/mission-worker.md:12-15 model gemini-2.5-pro, temperature 0.1, max_turns 100, timeout_mins 30
agents/mission-worker.md:127 requires a structured JSON handoff
Frontmatter tool validation via python script confirmed read_file, write_file, replace, run_shell_command, grep_search, and list_directory are all present

assertions.title: Code quality worker agent has correct definition
assertions.reason: agents/code-quality-worker.md has the required frontmatter fields, required tool set, and a body centered on code review/refactoring with a structured handoff protocol.
evidence: agents/code-quality-worker.md:12-15 model gemini-2.5-pro, temperature 0.1, max_turns 60, timeout_mins 20
agents/code-quality-worker.md:125 requires a structured JSON handoff
Frontmatter tool validation via python script confirmed read_file, write_file, replace, run_shell_command, grep_search, and list_directory are all present

assertions.title: Worker agents include init.sh execution instructions
assertions.reason: Both worker agent bodies instruct the agent to run .mission/init.sh if it exists before implementation/review work and explicitly say to run it once at the start of the session.
evidence: agents/mission-worker.md:48-54 says to execute bash .mission/init.sh if it exists and to run it once at the start of the session, not per feature
agents/code-quality-worker.md:47-53 says to execute bash .mission/init.sh if it exists and to run it once at the start of the session

Shell existence check on /Users/codex/Projects/gemini-mission-control-extension/.mission/init.sh returned non-zero, confirming the assertion was validated from agent instructions rather than an existing repo script

frictions: Repository root does not currently contain README.md; used GEMINI.md as the equivalent top-level documentation surface after confirming project structure.
The repo does not currently have a .mission/init.sh file, so VAL-WORK-008 was validated against the required worker-agent instructions rather than by executing init.sh.
summary: Validated 3 worker-dispatch assertions on the real repository surface. VAL-WORK-001, VAL-WORK-002, and VAL-WORK-008 all passed based on direct inspection of agents/mission-worker.md and agents/code-quality-worker.md plus shell-backed frontmatter checks.
